title: Non-record: LoRA TTT exploration on SOTA base (negative result)
body:
## Summary

Explored combining LoRA Test-Time Training (TTT) with the current SOTA (thwu1 Int5-MLP + BigramHash + SWA, 1.1428 BPB). TTT adapts per-document during evaluation using rank-8 LoRA adapters on Q/V projections.

**Key finding: TTT does NOT improve SOTA.** Documented as informative negative result for the community.

## Experimental Results (1xH100)

| Method | val_bpb | Delta |
|--------|---------|-------|
| Standard eval | 1.1734 | baseline |
| TTT (chunk=256, seq=1024) | 1.3117 | +0.138 (worse) |
| TTT (chunk=256, seq=2048) | 1.3366 | +0.163 (worse) |

## Why TTT Fails on SOTA

1. **Context loss**: SOTA trains with seq_len=2048. TTT chunks into 256-token segments with 1024 window, losing long-range context
2. **Redundant local signals**: SmearGate + BigramHash already inject strong bigram context into embeddings
3. **Document boundary artifacts**: LoRA resets between documents, barely trains on short FineWeb docs

## Code Changes

Minimal modifications to train_gpt.py:
- Added LoRA support to CausalSelfAttention, Block, and GPT.forward
- Added BatchedLinearLoRA, BatchedTTTLoRA classes
- Added eval_val_ttt_lora evaluation function
- Standalone eval script (eval_ttt_only.py in experiments/)

## Takeaway

TTT is most effective on models with short context windows and weak local embeddings. On architectures that already maximize context (seq_len=2048) and inject strong bigram signals, TTT provides no additional benefit.

Built on PR #414 by @thwu1. TTT approach from @samacqua.

## Test plan
- [x] Smoke test 200 steps (1xH100)
- [x] 5108-step run with standard eval
- [x] 5108-step run with TTT eval (seq_len=1024)
- [x] 5108-step run with TTT eval (seq_len=2048)
- [ ] Full 8xH100 training run (pending compute credits)
